symptoms.symptom_name: Reproducible exertional calf pain relieved completely by rest (intermittent claudication)
symptoms.character: tight cramping bilateral calf pain on walking ~50 m

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fixed-distance exertional pain relieved by rest is the classic claudication signature.
DVT pain is constant, not exertional and rest-relieved.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute worsening over one week on a 6-month background

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid deterioration in claudication distance signals a tightening arterial supply and warrants urgent assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain relieved by standing still, not by sitting or spinal flexion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Relief on ceasing exertion (standing) rather than posture change points to arterial rather than neurogenic cause.
Neurogenic claudication requires sitting or lumbar flexion for relief, not simply stopping.

symptoms.symptom_name: No rest pain, night pain, ulceration, pallor, coldness or numbness at rest (6 Ps screened negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the 6 Ps on history indicates a currently viable limb, but does not fully exclude ischaemia without a pulse exam.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent bilateral dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses with weak femoral pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent distal pulses objectively confirm significant lower-limb arterial disease — a sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via telehealth.
Carotid bruits indicate systemic atherosclerotic burden and elevated stroke risk — a sealed clinician auscultation finding.
Tendon xanthomas are near-specific for familial hypercholesterolaemia — require palpation, sealed.
Xanthelasma and premature corneal arcus support a severe lipid disorder; these are video-observable by the patient.

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated LDL cholesterol (anticipated, not yet drawn)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of the lipid disorder and statin intensity cannot be quantified; familial hypercholesterolaemia goes uncharacterised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms and grades the underlying lipid disorder driving the atherosclerosis.
Borderline/raised blood pressure adds to the atherosclerotic risk profile; a clinician measurement, not obtainable reliably via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sedentary lifestyle, processed-food diet and overweight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Modifiable risk factors that accelerate atherosclerosis and are targets for behaviour-change management.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Diagnostic tetrad of severe lipid disorder
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tendon xanthomas, xanthelasma, corneal arcus and elevated LDL together are highly specific for familial hypercholesterolaemia and mandate high-intensity statin therapy and family cascade screening.
Fixed-distance exertional pain relieved by standing plus absent distal pulses is the diagnostic pattern of symptomatic PAD.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line objective confirmation and grading of peripheral arterial disease; guides revascularisation urgency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Arterial duplex ultrasound of the lower limbs
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Localises and grades stenoses and helps distinguish stable disease from a critically threatened limb.
Quantifies the severity of the lipid disorder and characterises probable familial hypercholesterolaemia before commencing high-intensity statin.
Screens for diabetes as a coexisting cardiovascular risk factor.
Baseline cardiac assessment given widespread atherosclerosis and high coronary risk.
The pattern is arterial, not venous — routine DVT work-up is not indicated and would misdirect management.
